# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# Declare variables for the Deployment Manager
resources:
- name: myInstance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/my_project/zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
     boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/my_project/global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
        name: External NAT
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: my-service-account@my_project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
      scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/devstorage.read_write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/logging.write
